title: Multnomah County Winter & Severe Weather Resources - 211info
text: Last Updated: January 22, 2026 Not open tonight: Emergency severe weather shelters. Open today/tonight: Existing year-round shelters, overnight-only shelters, and daytime drop-in centers (see below). To find warming spaces and shelters, please see this interactive map of shelter locations and warming sites . To find other cold weather resources like warm clothes, hot meals, and transportation options, call 211. When does Multnomah County Open Warming Resources? Care for When It’s Cold – Multnomah County Warming Resources During periods of extreme cold weather, 211info partners with Multnomah County to share resource information and provide transportation options. Anyone seeking shelter should contact 211info. 211info staff are available to identify shelters, warming centers, and review transportation options for Multnomah County. Stay Informed: Sign Up for Alerts Sign up to receive text alerts from 211info when a Weather Advisory is called in Multnomah County, TEXT “pdxshelter” to 898211 Sign up to receive email alerts from 211info when a Weather Advisory is called in Multnomah County WINTER SHELTER No seasonal shelters available at this time. See below for available shelters and drop-in centers. To find other cold weather resources like warm clothes, hot meals, and transportation options, call 211. YEAR-ROUND OVERNIGHT SHELTERS Locate year-round shelters YEAR-ROUND DAYTIME DROP-IN CENTERS Locate year-round daytime drop-in centers For Service Providers If your agency offers winter or severe weather services and you need to update the information in our database, please contact the 211info Resource Department at [email protected] . Volunteering For information about volunteering at emergency severe weather shelters, visit the Multnomah County volunteering page .
